context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 code_contribution CC024

decision.description: Created TRACE (Transparent Research AI Collaboration Environment) protocol and template for documenting AI-human collaboration in scientific research
decision.rationale:
Need a systematic way to track AI contributions for the academic paper on AI-assisted science methodology. TRACE enables tracking code authorship, idea provenance, error attribution, and human interventions with computed metrics for publication.

Alternatives considered:
  - ARC (AI Research Chronicle): TRACE better emphasizes the tracing/tracking function
  - PARS (Provenance-Aware Research System): TRACE is more intuitive
  - Research Ledger: Doesn't explicitly reference AI collaboration
  - SciProv: TRACE has better branding potential

Confidence: 0.85

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 decision D001

decision.description: Target ACM Creativity and Cognition 2026 conference for the empirical AI-art paper, reserving top-tier journals (Nature Human Behaviour, PNAS Nexus) for a future methods paper specifically on consensus UMAP with MLP projection head
decision.rationale:
User is a PhD candidate with novel methods (consensus UMAP + MLP projection head) that deserve their own methods paper. The empirical AI-art findings fit well with the art-tech niche audience at ACM C&C, while the methodological contributions could be separated into a higher-impact methods paper applying across multiple domains. This strategy maximizes publication value from the work.

Confidence: 0.7

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 decision D002

annotation.content:
Split research into two papers: (1) empirical AI-art paper for ACM C&C 2026 targeting art-tech audience, (2) methods paper on consensus UMAP + MLP projection head for top-tier journal targeting computational social science / NLP methods audience

Triggered by: User mentioned wanting to write a separate methods paper within the next month for dissertation
Status: pending
Type: approach

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 code_contribution CC001

annotation.content:
ACM C&C 2026 accepts papers up to 8,000 words (excluding refs/captions), requires anonymous submission using acmart template with manuscript,review,anonymous documentclass options, and mandates in-person presentation in London. Deadline: Feb 5, 2026.

Evidence: Web search of cc.acm.org/2026 conference website and papers submission guidelines
Confidence: high (0.9)

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 learning L001

decision.description: Submit empirical AI-art paper to ACM Creativity and Cognition 2026 (deadline Feb 5, 2026); reserve consensus UMAP + MLP projection head methodology for separate high-impact methods paper
decision.rationale:
User has high risk tolerance but recognizes C&C is strategically appropriate for the domain-specific empirical story. The methods paper will be strengthened by demonstrating generalizability across 3 domains rather than being buried in a single-domain application. C&C provides solid publication while preserving methodological novelty for maximum impact.

Confidence: 0.7

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 code_contribution CC002

decision.description: Add explicit tracking for manual human edits in TRACE protocol: When human edits files directly (outside AI session), they should log this via trace_log_code with human_authored_lines and a description tag [HUMAN-MANUAL-EDIT]
decision.rationale:
Current TRACE implementation cannot automatically detect manual file edits. Human must self-report these changes. This maintains attribution accuracy for scientific transparency.

Confidence: 0.7

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 decision D004

annotation.content:
PROBLEM: LaTeX \Description command undefined when compiling NHB manuscript - ACM accessibility macro not available in standard article class

SOLUTION: Add stub command: \newcommand{\Description}[1]{} at document preamble

Severity: medium

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 gotcha G001

annotation.content:
PROBLEM: naturemag bibliography style not found when compiling LaTeX - style file not in standard distributions

SOLUTION: Use plainnat instead: \bibliographystyle{plainnat} - works with natbib and provides author-year citations

Severity: medium

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 gotcha G002

annotation.content:
PROBLEM: TRACE attribution was logging ALL changes as ai_authored_lines, even when human directed the specific change - misattributes intellectual contribution

SOLUTION: Distinguish: human_authored = human provides exact text OR directs specific change; ai_authored = AI suggests content that human approves. Use [HUMAN-EDIT] git tag for manual edits.

Severity: critical

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 gotcha G003

annotation.content:
PROBLEM: Cannot automatically detect manual human file edits - TRACE only logs changes made during Claude sessions

SOLUTION: Use git commit tags ([HUMAN-EDIT], [AI-ASSIST], [COLLAB]) and trace_sync_git to parse history. Human reports manual edits at session start or uses tagged commits.

Severity: high

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 gotcha G004

annotation.content:
Nature Human Behaviour, New Media & Society, and Big Data & Society all explicitly accept preprints on arXiv/SocArXiv before submission - no scoop risk from posting preprint first

Evidence: Web search of journal preprint policies
Confidence: high (0.9)

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 learning L003

annotation.content:
Git integration is essential for automated TRACE attribution - commit message tags ([HUMAN-EDIT], [AI-ASSIST]) enable zero-friction logging without manual reporting

Evidence: Implemented trace_sync.py and successfully synced 5 historical commits automatically
Confidence: high (0.9)

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 learning L004

annotation.content:
Word-level tracking (not just lines) is important for text/manuscript files - a 1-line edit could be 3 words or 30 words, and word counts matter for understanding intellectual contribution in writing

Evidence: User requested word tracking after seeing line-only metrics didn't capture granularity of abstract edits
Confidence: high (0.9)

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 learning L005

annotation.content:
Section-level attribution (intro vs methods vs results vs discussion) would reveal collaboration patterns - e.g., AI might contribute more to methods writing while humans do more discussion editing

Evidence: User suggestion during metadata extension discussion
Confidence: medium (0.7)

context.reasoning_summary: Migrated from TRACE v1.0 learning L006

annotation.content:
Smart TRACE triggers work best as a hybrid of behavioral instructions (in CLAUDE.md) plus a checkpoint tool (trace_knowledge_check) - pure automation is complex and brittle, while pure manual logging misses things

Evidence: Implemented both approaches; behavioral triggers guide proactive logging while checkpoint tool catches what's missed
Confidence: high (0.9)
